title: Lossless power conversion to dc method and device
abstract: A device and a method for power conversion to DC using zero-current and/or zero- voltage switching. Provides output voltage of any level and polarity. Operates with any input voltage, including DC and AC. Power loss is substantially eliminated. The conversion efficiency is independent of load consumption, from the idle to the maximum power. The EMI level is very low. Provides for input to output isolation without use of transformers.
claims:
Claims 1. A method of energy transfer between a storage capacitor (C3) and a source of an input voltage (VIN), connected to an input terminal pair (IN), comprising at least one of: (A) a charging mode, comprising, while a modulus of a third voltage (V3) across the storage capacitor (C3) being less than a modulus of a nominal voltage (V3N), cycling through: (a) making a first loop comprising the input terminal pair (IN), a dosing coil (L1 ), and a dosing capacitor (C1 ), connected in series with each other; (b) detecting a first condition of a first current (11 ) in the dosing coil (L1 ) becoming non-zero; (c) detecting a second condition of the first current (11 ) becoming substantially zero, thereupon breaking the first loop; (d) making a second loop comprising the dosing capacitor (C1 ), a replenishing coil (L3), and the storage capacitor (C3), connected in series with each other; (e) detecting a third condition of a first voltage (V1 ) across the dosing capacitor (C1 ) becoming substantially zero, thereupon making a third loop comprising the storage capacitor (C3) and the replenishing coil (L3), and breaking the second loop; and (f) detecting a fourth condition of a third current (13) in the replenishing coil (L3) becoming substantially zero, thereupon breaking the third loop; and (B) an uncharging mode, comprising in the following order: discontinuing the cycling on completion of step (f); (g) making the third loop; (h) detecting a condition of the third voltage (V3) becoming substantially zero, thereupon making the second loop and breaking the third loop; (i) detecting the fourth condition, thereupon breaking the second loop; (j) making the first loop; (k) detecting the third condition, thereupon shorting the dosing capacitor (C1 ); and (I) detecting the second condition, thereupon breaking the first loop. 2. The method according to Claim 1 wherein the dosing coil (L1 ) and the replenishing coil (L3) are a same coil. 3. The method according to Claim 1 further comprising: (A) isolating the storage capacitor (C3) from the dosing capacitor (C1 ) in the charging mode during steps (a) through (c), and in the uncharging mode during steps (j) through (I); and (B) isolating the input terminal pair (IN) from the dosing capacitor (C1 ) in the charging mode during steps (d) through (f), and in the uncharging mode during steps (g) through (i). 4. The method according to Claim 1 further comprising a sequence: making a fourth loop comprising the dosing coil (L1 ) and the dosing capacitor (C1 ); detecting the first condition; and detecting the second condition, thereupon breaking the fourth loop; the sequence being performed: (A) in the charging mode, between steps (c) and (d), if 

 polarities of the first voltage (V1 ) and the nominal voltage (V3N) are opposite to each other; and (B) in the uncharging mode, between steps (i) and (j), if polarities of the first voltage (V1 ) and the input voltage (VIN) are opposite to each other. 5. The method according to Claim 1 further comprising the step of interchanging terminals of the dosing capacitor (C1 ); said step being performed (A) in the charging mode, between steps (c) and (d), if polarities of the first voltage (V1 ) and the nominal voltage (V3N) are opposite to each other; and (B) in the uncharging mode, between steps (i) and 0). if polarities of the first voltage (V1 ) and the input voltage (VIN) are opposite to each other. 6. A method of energy transfer between a storage capacitor (C3) and a source of an input voltage (VIN), connected to an input terminal pair (IN), comprising at least one of: (A) a charging mode, comprising, while a modulus of a third voltage (V3) across the storage capacitor (C3) being less than a modulus of a nominal voltage (V3N), cycling through: (a) making a first loop comprising the input terminal pair (IN), a dosing coil (L1 ), and a dosing capacitor (C1 ), connected in series with each other; (b) detecting a first condition of a first current (11 ) in the dosing coil (L1 ) becoming non-zero; (c) detecting a second condition of the first current (11 ) becoming substantially zero, thereupon breaking the first loop; (d) making a second loop comprising the dosing capacitor (C1 ) and a raising coil (L2); (e) detecting a third condition of a first voltage (V1 ) across the dosing capacitor (C1 ) becoming substantially zero, thereupon making a third loop comprising the raising coil (L2) and a raising capacitor (C2), and breaking the second loop; (f) detecting a condition of: polarities of the nominal voltage (V3N) and a second voltage (V2) across the raising capacitor (C2) being a same polarity, and a second current (I2) in the raising coil (L2) becoming substantially zero, thereupon breaking the third loop; (g) making a fourth loop comprising the raising capacitor (C2), a replenishing coil (L3) and the storage capacitor (C3), connected in series with each other; (h) detecting a fourth condition of the second voltage (V2) becoming substantially zero, thereupon making a fifth loop comprising the replenishing coil (L3) and the storage capacitor (C3), and breaking the fourth loop; and (i) detecting a fifth condition of a third current (I3) in the replenishing coil (L3) becoming substantially zero, thereupon breaking the fifth loop; and (B) an uncharging mode, comprising in the following order: discontinuing the cycling on completion of step (i); (j) making the fifth loop; (k) detecting a condition of the third voltage (V3) becoming substantially zero, thereupon making the fourth loop and breaking the fifth loop; (I) detecting the fifth condition, thereupon 

 breaking the fourth loop; (m) making the third loop; (n) detecting the fourth condition, thereupon making the second loop and breaking the third loop; (o) detecting a condition of the first voltage (V1 ) and the input voltage (VIN) being of a same polarity, and the first current (11 ) becoming substantially zero, thereupon breaking the second loop; (p) making the first loop; (q) detecting the third condition, thereupon shorting the dosing capacitor (C1 ); and (r) detecting the second condition, thereupon breaking the first loop. 7. The method according to Claim 6 wherein at least two of: the dosing coil (L1 ), the raising coil (L2), and the replenishing coil (L3), are a same coil. 8. The method according to Claim 6 wherein, in the first and second loops, the dosing (C1 ) and raising (C2) capacitors are connected in parallel with each other. 9. The method according to Claim 6 further comprising: (A) isolating the storage capacitor (C3) from the dosing capacitor (C1 ) in the charging mode during steps (a) through (c), and in the uncharging mode during steps (p) through (r); and (B) isolating the input terminal pair (IN) from the dosing capacitor (C1 ) in the charging mode during steps (d) through (i), and in the uncharging mode during steps (j) through (o). 10. A device for energy transfer between a storage capacitor (C3) and a source of an input voltage (VIN) comprising: a dosing coil (L1 ), a dosing capacitor (C1 ), a replenishing coil (L3), an input terminal pair (IN) for connection to the input voltage (VIN), a plurality of switches, and a controller; wherein: (a) the plurality of switches are coupled to said input terminal pair (IN), capacitors, and coils, and are configured to make and to break: a loop comprising the input terminal pair (IN), the dosing coil (L1 ), and the dosing capacitor (C1 ), connected in series with each other; a loop comprising the dosing capacitor (C1 ), the replenishing coil (L3), and the storage capacitor (C3), connected in series with each other; and a loop comprising the replenishing coil (L3) and the storage capacitor (C3); (b) the controller is coupled to said loops and is configured to detect the conditions of: a first current (11 ) in the dosing coil (L1 ) becoming non-zero; the first current (11 ) becoming substantially zero; a first voltage (V1 ) across the dosing capacitor (C1 ) becoming substantially zero; a third current (I3) in the replenishing coil (L3) becoming substantially zero; and a modulus of a third voltage (V3) across the storage capacitor (C3) being less than a modulus of a nominal voltage (V3N); and (c) the controller is operably coupled to the plurality of switches, and configured to control them according to said conditions. 11. The device according to claim 10 wherein the dosing coil (L1 ) and the replenishing coil (L3) are a same coil. 12. The device according to claim 10 wherein: the plurality of switches are further configured to short and unshort the dosing capacitor (C1 ); and the controller is further configured to detect a condition of the third voltage (V3) becoming substantially zero. 13. The device according to claim 10 wherein: (A) the plurality of switches are further configured to provide: (i) isolation of the input terminal pair (IN) from the dosing capacitor (C1 ), and (ii) isolation of the storage capacitor (C3) from the dosing capacitor (C1 ); and (B) the controller is further configured to continuously maintain at least one of (i) and (ii). 14. The device according to claim 10 wherein: (A) the plurality of switches are further configured to make and to break a loop comprising the dosing capacitor (C1 ) and the dosing coil (L1 ); and (B) the controller is further configured to detect at least one of the conditions: (i) polarities of the first voltage (V1 ) and of the nominal voltage (V3N) being opposite to each other; and (ii) polarities of the first voltage (V1 ) and of the input voltage (VIN) being opposite to each other. 15. The device according to claim 10 wherein: (A) the plurality of switches are further configured to interchange terminals of the dosing capacitor (C1 ); and (B) the controller is further configured to detect at least one of the conditions: (i) polarities of the first voltage (V1 ) and of the nominal voltage (V3N) being opposite to each other; and (ii) polarities of the first voltage (V1 ) and of the input voltage (VIN) being opposite to each other. 16. A device for energy transfer between a storage capacitor (C3) and a source of an input voltage (VIN) comprising: a dosing coil (L1 ), a dosing capacitor (C1 ), a raising coil (L2), a raising capacitor (C2), a replenishing coil (L3), an input terminal pair (IN) for connection to the input voltage (VIN), a plurality of switches, and a controller; wherein: (a) the plurality of switches are coupled to said input terminal pair (IN), capacitors, and coils, and are configured to make and to break: a first loop comprising the input terminal pair (IN), the dosing coil (L1 ), and the dosing capacitor (C1 ), connected in series with each other; a second loop comprising the dosing capacitor (C1 ) and the raising coil (L2); a loop comprising the raising coil (L2) and the raising capacitor (C2); a loop comprising the raising capacitor (C2), the replenishing coil (L3), and the storage capacitor (C3), connected in series with each other; and a loop comprising the replenishing coil (L3) and the storage capacitor (C3); (b) the controller is coupled to said loops and is configured to detect the conditions of: a first current ( 11 ) in the dosing coil (L1 ) becoming non-zero; the first current (11 ) becoming substantially zero; a first voltage 

 (V1 ) across the dosing capacitor (C1 ) becoming substantially zero; a second current (I2) in the raising coil (L2) becoming substantially zero; a second voltage (V2) across the raising capacitor (C2) becoming substantially zero; polarities of the second voltage (V2) and of a nominal voltage (V3N) being a same polarity; a third current (13) in the replenishing coil (L3) becoming substantially zero; and a modulus of a third voltage (V3) across the storage capacitor (C3) being less than a modulus of the nominal voltage (V3N); and (c) the controller is operably coupled to the plurality of switches, and configured to control them according to said conditions. 17. The device according to claim 16 wherein at least two of: the dosing coil (L1 ), the raising coil (L2), and the replenishing coil (L3), are a same coil. 18. The device according to claim 16 wherein, in the first and second loops, the raising capacitor (C2) is connected in parallel with the dosing capacitor (C1 ). 19. The device according to claim 16 wherein: (A) the plurality of switches are further configured to short and unshort the dosing capacitor (C1 ); and (B) the controller is further configured to detect the conditions of: (i) the third voltage (V3) becoming substantially zero; and (ii) polarities of the first voltage (V1 ) and of the input voltage (VIN) being a same polarity. 20. The device according to claim 16 wherein (A) the plurality of switches are further configured to provide: (i) isolation of the input terminal pair (IN) from the dosing capacitor (C1 ), and (ii) isolation of the storage capacitor (C3) from the raising capacitor (C2); and (B) the controller is further configured to continuously maintain at least one of (i) and (ii).
